channel.title: Thiccctionary
channel.description: A satirical daily dictionary of objects of unusual girth. Strictly things, never people.

item.title: tugboat
item.description: A sturdy, compact watercraft designed for towing or pushing larger vessels, often characterized by its robust hull and unapologetic rearward girth.

item.title: Cathedral, Concrete Brutalist
item.description: A monumental ecclesiastical structure characterized by its unapologetically bulky concrete forms and stark geometric lines; typically possessing a cavernous interior that suggests a sanctuary for contemplation and echoes.

item.title: Watermelons, Seedless Giant
item.description: A larger-than-life specimen of Citrullus lanatus, engineered to forgo the traditional reproductive encumbrances, resulting in an unbroken expanse of juicy grandeur. Best appreciated when viewed from a low angle, where its ground-level dominance becomes manifest.

item.title: [Article] On the Forty-Five-Foot Soccer Ball, Which Is Visible From Aircraft
item.description: The Thiccc Beat: Theodore Vance on Massport's 45-foot soccer ball, East Boston (record attempt vs 38 ft 11.8 in, Doha 2013). Ruling: Thiccc (pending senior review).

item.title: Pickup Truck, Ford F-250
item.description: A robust expression of vehicular heft, characterized by its imposing grille and the capacity to tow entire weekends. Especially notable when parked in tight urban quarters, where it seems to negotiate cobblestones with an innate diplomacy.

item.title: [Article] Filed Replies, Vol. 3
item.description: Bartholomew Whitmore, Senior Cataloguer, replies to a handful of letters. Vol. 3.

item.title: Pickup Truck, F-250
item.description: A robust vehicle of formidable mass, characterized by its dual rear wheels and a cab configuration that seems to defy the laws of aerodynamics.

item.title: [Article] Chunk. A Brief and Undignified Loss of Composure.
item.description: The Thiccc Beat: Spider Hennessy on Fat Bear Week 2025 champion, Bear 32 "Chunk", Katmai National Park. Ruling: Thiccc.

item.title: [Article] A Floating City Files Its Paperwork
item.description: The Thiccc Beat: Bertram Whitmore on Star of the Seas (Royal Caribbean, Icon-class). Ruling: Thiccc.

item.title: [Article] On Whether a 2,819-Pound Pumpkin Is Still, Strictly, a Pumpkin
item.description: The Thiccc Beat: Bartholomew Whitmore on 2,819 lb world-record pumpkin (Paton brothers, UK) + 2,346 lb US champion (Brandon Dawson). Ruling: Thiccc.

item.title: Giant Watermelon, Sugar Baby
item.description: A cultivar of Citrullus lanatus known for its rotundity and deep green rind with dark stripes, often displayed with pride at county fair weigh-ins. Its posterior, viewed astern, defies casual description.

item.title: Savoy Cabbage
item.description: A leafy vegetable of distinguished girth, characterized by its densely-packed, undulating leaves and a verdant hue that shifts subtly in raking light.

item.title: Antoine Ferris Wheel
item.description: A large amusement ride characterized by its prominent circular structure and multiple passenger cabins, each offering panoramic views that defy casual description when viewed astern in raking light.

item.title: Mooring Rope, Naval Hawser
item.description: A formidable cable of considerable girth, typically employed to anchor ships securely to their berths, its sheer mass exudes a gravitational pull of its own.

item.title: [Article] Filed Replies, Vol. 2
item.description: Bartholomew Whitmore, Senior Cataloguer, replies to a handful of letters. Vol. 2.

item.title: Boeing 777X
item.description: A long-haul aircraft renowned for its expansive width and fuselage curvature, especially when observed from behind in dramatic lighting.

item.title: Combine Harvester, John Deere
item.description: A robust agricultural machine manufactured by John Deere, characterized by its substantial rear housing and impressive grain capacity, especially when viewed from behind in flattering sunlight.

item.title: Tractor, Compact Utility
item.description: A four-wheeled agricultural vehicle of moderate horsepower, built low and wide for orchard work, hobby farms, and the kind of unspecified yard task that a homeowner believes requires diesel.

item.title: Hippopotamus, Common
item.description: A semi-aquatic mammal whose substantial mass and low center of gravity render it a prime exemplar of thicccness in nature; especially when viewed astern, its posterior defies casual description.

item.title: [Article] From the Boat, Issue 1
item.description: Bertram Whitmore, Publisher, writes from the Margaret IV. Vol. 1.

item.title: [Article] Filed Replies, Vol. 1
item.description: Bartholomew Whitmore, Senior Cataloguer, replies to a handful of letters. Vol. 1.

item.title: Sequoia, General Sherman
item.description: A giant sequoia tree renowned for its extraordinary mass and height, particularly when viewed from ground level where the sky becomes a mere afterthought.

item.title: Ever Given, Suez Container Ship
item.description: A container vessel of formidable displacement, noted for its capacity to momentarily rearrange global trade routes. Specifically, a ship whose breadth challenges traditional canal dimensions.

item.title: Pumpkin, Mammoth Gold
item.description: An especially large cultivar of pumpkin renowned for its substantial mass and radiant hues; typically exhibited with pride at autumnal gatherings and agricultural fairs.

item.title: Cement Truck, BetaMax
item.description: A cement mixer of considerable bulk, noted for its rotund drum and dedication to gravitational permanence; especially when viewed in profile, the subject's posterior challenges architectural norms.

item.title: Boulder, Glacial Erratic
item.description: A substantial rock formation, often resting incongruously upon a landscape, relocated by ancient glacial activity, notable for its commanding presence when viewed astern in natural light.

item.title: Crankshaft, Marine Diesel
item.description: A stout, elongated component of maritime propulsion systems, noted for its formidable girth and rotational authority. Particularly evident when viewed astern, in raking light.

item.title: Anvil, Blacksmith
item.description: A dense, industrial-grade tool upon which metal is worked, characterized by its substantial mass and steadfast refusal to budge without considerable effort. Frequently sighted with a patina of history and dust, typically under raking light.

item.title: Silo, Grain Concrete
item.description: A towering agricultural structure characterized by its substantial cylindrical form and ability to outshine any pastoral landscape with its commanding presence.

item.title: Atlas Stone, Strongman
item.description: A spherical weight of imposing mass, employed in the sport of strongman competitions, often requiring Herculean effort to lift and maneuver. Its surface is characteristically rough, resembling a cosmic boulder that defies trivial engagement.

item.title: Tabular Iceberg, Antarctic
item.description: A colossal slab of ice, characterized by its flat top and steep sides, often mistaken for a floating geological feature. Its mass and breadth defy casual navigation, esp. when viewed in stark contrast against the horizon.

item.title: Kettlebell, Competition
item.description: A spherical weight of iron or steel, characterized by a single-looped handle, used in competitive sports for its standardized mass and satisfyingly stout profile.

item.title: Cabbage, Savoy
item.description: A botanical heavyweight of the brassica family, renowned for its deeply crinkled leaves and substantial presence in the garden; esp. one whose verdant layers suggest a rotational symmetry best admired in morning light.

item.title: Grand Piano, Concert
item.description: A substantial, resonant keyboard instrument, designed for symphonic performances, characterized by its elongated, curvaceous body that demands both reverence and a spacious venue.

item.title: Tuba, Contrabass
item.description: A wind instrument of considerable girth, often described as a brass behemoth with a voluminous bell, especially when viewed astern in raking light.

item.title: [Article] Girth in Service of Purpose: The Thiccc Thing That Works
item.description: The Thiccctionary editorial board argues that the highest order of thicccness is not decorative but load-bearing, girth as the engine of purpose itself.

item.title: Transformer, Power Generation
item.description: A hefty apparatus in an electrical substation, distinguished by its imposing stature and multi-colored coils; esp. one viewed astern, where its girth suggests a surplus of voltage.

item.title: Kettledrum, Industrial
item.description: A percussion instrument of imposing drum diameter, noted for its resonant shell and industrial-grade stoutness; esp. one observed in profile under stage lighting.

item.title: Dumper Truck, Articulated
item.description: A robust off-road vehicle equipped with a hinged joint between the cab and the dump box, allowing for enhanced maneuverability on uneven terrain.

item.title: Cement Mixer, Industrial
item.description: A vehicular colossus of the concrete world, characterized by its ample rotating drum and the undeniable gravitas it brings to any construction site.

item.title: Bagger 288
item.description: A leviathan of earth-moving machinery, noteworthy for its massive revolving bucket wheel that shovels earth with an unhurried inevitability; esp. one observed from a distance, silhouetted against the horizon as an engineering colossus.

item.title: [Article] The Physics of Thiccc: Why Big Things Stay Big
item.description: Square-cube law, structural necessity, and the engineering logic behind why truly large objects look the way they do. A field guide to the geometry of girth.

item.title: [Article] The Bagger 288: The Thiccc-est Machine on Earth
item.description: At 96 metres tall and 220 metres long, the Bagger 288 is the largest land vehicle ever built. A field report on what it means to be truly, structurally, irreversibly thiccc.

item.title: [Article] Foods of Substance: A Thiccc Survey
item.description: From Parmigiano wheels to heritage tomatoes to Atlantic Giant pumpkins, when food's structural commitment becomes its identity.

item.title: Spruce, Suburban
item.description: A mature evergreen specimen (genus Picea) cultivated as a residential or municipal landscape feature; characteristically pyramidal, with branches sweeping low to the ground in a dense conical mass.

item.title: Hoover Dam
item.description: A concrete arch-gravity dam spanning the Black Canyon of the Colorado River, completed 1936; 726 feet from foundation to crest, 660 feet of base thickness tapering to 45 feet at the crown.

item.title: Whale, Blue
item.description: A marine leviathan whose girth and length transcend terrestrial metrics, often seen astern with a raking light that accentuates its oceanic immensity.

item.title: Wheel, Parmigiano-Reggiano
item.description: A cylindrical mass of aged cheese, notable for its robust curvature and dense, granular interior; often showcased in markets and delicatessens worldwide.

item.title: Watermelon, Moon and Stars
item.description: A cultivar of watermelon renowned for its celestial patterning of yellow spots and larger orbs set against a dark green rind, reminiscent of a night sky.

item.title: [Article] The Anatomy of a Thiccc Vehicle
item.description: Five parts worth knowing: wheelbase, aft, dually, cargo bay, kin. A field guide to the parts of a thiccc vehicle.

item.title: [Article] Concrete: A Material Study
item.description: The material behind cement mixers, Brutalism, and Hoover Dam. Why concrete forces thicccness on everything it builds.

item.title: Sofa, Chesterfield
item.description: A capacious upholstered seating apparatus characterized by its deep button-tufting and rolled arms; esp. one whose presence becomes a room's anchor furniture by physical fact.

item.title: [Article] Thiccc Things You Walk Past Every Day
item.description: Fire hydrants, mailboxes, bollards, dumpsters, HVAC, the unsung architecture of a sidewalk.

item.title: [Article] A Taxonomy of Thiccc Architecture
item.description: Defense, ideology, and mass housing. Three programs, one silhouette. Castle walls, Brutalism, Khrushchyovka.

item.title: [Article] The Inherent Thicccness of Industrial Scale
item.description: Why physics insists on the silhouette. Bagger 288, Saturn V, supertankers, Hoover Dam.

item.title: Banana, Cavendish
item.description: A cultivar of banana (Musa acuminata) renowned for its robust and curvaceous physique, often found lounging in fruit bowls and breakfast spreads across the globe.

item.title: [Article] Why Cement Trucks Are the Thiccc-est Industrial Vehicle
item.description: An argument, with engineering. The mixer is not in competition. It is the standard.

item.title: [Article] How to Spot a Thiccc Thing in the Wild: A Field Guide
item.description: Three tests. No instruments required. Silhouette, ratio, character.

item.title: Frigidaire, Side-by-Side
item.description: A domestic paragon of capacious elegance, featuring dual chilled chambers that stand in ostentatious parallel, prepared to preserve victuals with all the subtlety of a colossus.

item.title: [Article] The Five Thiccc-est Things We’ve Catalogued (So Far)
item.description: A working ranking. Heritage tomatoes to wide-body airliners. A hierarchy of girth.

item.title: [Article] What Counts as Thiccc? An Editorial Guide
item.description: The standing reference for what we’ll catalog and what we won’t. Includes the submission test.

item.title: [Article] A Brief History of Thiccc, the Word
item.description: From early-2000s message boards to mainstream dictionaries. With sources.

item.title: Thiccc Boeing
item.description: A widebody aircraft whose aft fuselage exhibits significantly more curvature than its fore fuselage; esp. one observed tail-on, in long-lens profile.

item.title: Avocado, Domestic
item.description: A pear-shaped fruit (Persea americana) of disproportionate top-to-bottom mass distribution, often photographed sliced for visual emphasis.

item.title: Ram 3500, Dually
item.description: A heavy-duty pickup truck of substantial posterior breadth, distinguished by dual rear wheels per side and an aggressively flared bedside that reads as architectural; esp. one photographed in golden-hour parking-lot stillness.

item.title: Heritage Tomato
item.description: A cultivar of tomato, often grown from seeds passed down through generations, characterized by its robust flavor and generous proportions.

item.title: Concrete Mixer
item.description: An industrial vehicle of contemplative rotundity, characterized by a single, slowly-rotating, drum-shaped midsection.

item.title: Pumpkin, Atlantic Giant
item.description: A cultivar of pumpkin renowned for its prodigious girth, cultivated primarily for competitive size and decorative impact.

item.title: Ship, Container Maersk
item.description: A maritime vessel characterized by its extensive breadth and formidable mass, designed to transport countless containers across the world's oceans; esp. one viewed astern, where its posterior expanse defies casual description.

item.title: Basilica, San Pedro
item.description: A monumental ecclesiastical structure in Vatican City, notable for its expansive nave and an apse that seems to embrace the horizon; its thiccc columns and domes defy casual description.

item.title: Ferris Wheel, London Eye
item.description: A monumental observation wheel situated on the South Bank of the River Thames, noted for its unparalleled rotundity and panoramic dominion over the skyline.

item.title: Reel, Cable Industrial
item.description: A large, cylindrical apparatus used for spooling extensive lengths of cable, notable for its substantial diameter and pronounced bulk when loaded to capacity.

item.title: Squash, Banana
item.description: A member of the Cucurbitaceae family, characterized by an elongated shape and a robust girth, particularly when viewed in profile against contrasting backgrounds. Often found lounging in suburban kitchen fruit bowls, it demands a second glance.

item.title: Stack, Pancakes Diner
item.description: A multi-layered assembly of pancakes, each disk contributing to a cumulative profile of impressive verticality and surface area, found predominantly in diner settings.

item.title: Pipe, Industrial Stack
item.description: A towering cylinder of engineered steel, designed for the expulsion of industrial emissions at a formidable height, often characterized by its unapologetic girth when viewed from any direction.

item.title: Tire, Tractor Rear
item.description: A formidable rubber component affixed to the rear axle of agricultural machinery, designed to convey a sense of unyielding stability and mass, particularly when observed from behind where its full breadth is undeniable.

item.title: Courgette, Giant Green
item.description: A specimen of the Cucurbita pepo species displaying astonishing lateral expansion, often challenging the structural integrity of standard garden beds.

item.title: Bell, Cathedral
item.description: A resonant structure of considerable mass, often found suspended in lofty ecclesiastical towers, its substantial profile best appreciated when viewed from a respectful distance.

item.title: Thickened Water
item.description: A pre-thickened drinking water formulated for individuals with dysphagia, sold in nectar, honey, and pudding consistencies per the IDDSI framework. Drunk slowly. Reviewed with respect.

item.title: Hot air balloon
item.description: A lighter-than-air vessel characterized by its bulbous fabric envelope and pronounced vertical displacement; esp. one observed astern, with the basket's footprint dwarfed by its own rotundity.

item.title: Tarantula
item.description: A spider of formidable girth, notably characterized by its substantial abdomen and robust leg span; often observed in repose, casting unusually expansive shadows.
